name: rad-session
description: Three-phase Claude Code workflow lifecycle with cross-machine continuity and slim wrapup: /init bootstraps a project once (deterministic stack detection via Python scripts, CLAUDE.md scaffold, gitignore handling for session files, rad-* plugin recommendations), /startup orients each session (Phase 0 git pull --ff-only with cross-machine handoff detection, parallel-batched resource discovery, stale-handoff auto-refresh from git log), /wrapup captures each session (recency-bounded conversation tagging, structured HANDOFF.md with 'what NOT to do' field, mechanical session-log derivation from HANDOFF, CLAUDE.md prune auto-skipped when unchanged, Phase 6 auto-commit + prompted push of session files with --push/--no-push/--quick overrides), /add-resource registers tools any time. Two Python scripts (detect-stack.py, detect-resources.py) make stack and MCP detection deterministic. PreCompact hook prevents silent context loss. 3.2 cuts wrapup wall time substantially without losing fidelity.
